title: chrysanthemum flavovirens var. stapfianum
intro: Chrysanthemum flavovirens var. stapfianum, also known as the Yellow-flowered Chrysanthemum or Stapf's Chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and inflammation, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe the nervous system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Chrysanthemum flavovirens var. stapfianum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
